title: Newcastle & Man Utd plan Jackson contingency - Wednesday's gossip
content:
Daily Star back page

Daily Mirror back page

Daily Express back page

The Times back page

The i back page

Daily Telegraph sports section

1 of 6

Chelsea striker Nicolas Jackson is seen as a back-up option for Manchester United and Newcastle, depending on who misses out on RB Leipzig forward Benjamin Sesko, Sunderland want Dominic Calvert-Lewin, while West Ham eye Aston Villa's Jacob Ramsey.

BothManchester UnitedandNewcastlehave earmarkedChelsea'sSenegal striker Nicolas Jackson, 24, as a contingency plan if they miss out onRB Leipzig'sSlovenia forward Benjamin Sesko.(The Athletic - subscription required),external

There is a growing feeling that 22-year-old Sesko wants to join United.(Sky Sports),external

Aston Villaare alsobracing themselves for interest fromManchester Unitedin England striker Ollie Watkins, 29,  if the Red Devils fail to complete a deal for Sesko.

RB Leipzighave contactedLiverpoolover the potential signing of English midfielder Harvey Elliott, 22, with the Reds wanting Â£40m plus a buy-back option, or above Â£50m without one.(The Athletic - subscription required),external

Manchester Unitedhave rejected five offers from clubs in the Premier League and Italy for 32-year-old England defender Harry Maguire.(Mail),external

Juventusare open to selling English centre-back Lloyd Kelly, 26, and regardArsenal'sPoland defender Jakub Kiwior, 25, as a potential replacement.(Gazzetta dello Sport - in Italian),external

Sunderlandare keen to sign formerEvertonstriker Dominic Calvert-Lewin on a free transfer, although the 28-year-old Englishman wants to wait to see if any other Premier League clubs are interested.(Talksport),external

West Hamremain interested inAston Villa'sJacob Ramsey, 24, with the English midfielder yet to make a decision on his long-term future.(Mail),external

Argentina winger Alejandro Garnacho will leaveManchester Unitedthis month, withChelsea, Aston VillaandTottenhamall interested in the 21-year-old.(TBR Football),external

Liverpoolhave been looking at a number of young centre-backs across Europe, includingParma's18-year-old Italian Giovanni Leoni.(Times - subscription required),external

Newcastleare set to approachAC Milanfor 23-year-old Germany defender Malick Thiaw.(Fabrizio Romano),external

Nottingham Forestwill hold talks with the agent ofJuventusand Brazil midfielder Douglas Luiz as they attempt to complete a deal for the 27-year-old.(Football Italia),external

RB Salzburg'sDanish midfielder Mads Bidstrup, 24, topsNottingham Forest'sshortlist of transfer targets.(Florian Plettenberg),external

FormerManchester Unitedleft-back Brandon Williams, 24, is wanted by several Championship clubs, includingHull City, despite being without a club for 12 months.(Mail),external

Derby Countyare closing in on the signing of formerLiverpoolmidfielder Bobby Clark, with the 20-year-old Englishman set to join the Rams on a season-long loan fromRB Salzburg.(Sky Sports),external

Tottenhamwant to add to their attacking options and haveAC Milan'sPortugal forward Rafael Leao, 26, and 24-year-oldReal Madridand Brazil winger Rodrygo among their targets.(GiveMeSport),external

Follow your club with BBC Sport

Preston North Endhave agreed a deal to sign 17-year-old forward Shay Reid from Irish Premiership clubCliftonville.

The Irish teenager has also been linked withAberdeen,CelticandLuton Town.(Belfast Telegraph),external

Hull Cityare facing competition for the signature of left-back Brandon Williams.

The 24-year-old Englishman has been without a club for a year since leavingManchester United.(Daily Mail),external

Sheffield Unitedare interested in signingMalmoandSwedendefender Nils Zatterstrom, 20.(The 72),external

Leicester Cityand Nigeria midfielder Wilfred Ndidi, 28, is set to join Turkish Superlig sideBesiktasfor Â£9m.(OneFootball),external

Blackpoolare set to beatHull Cityto the signing of formerMillwallforward Duncan Watmore, 31.(Football League World),external

Wycombe Wanderersare set to complete the signing ofBristol Rovers'English defender Connor Taylor, 23, in a deal worth more than Â£500,000.(BBC Sport)

Colchester Unitedare eyeing a move for formerNorthampton Townleft-back Ali Koiki, 25. The Londoner is currently on trial atCheltenham Town.(The 72),external
